name: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper. Creamy without any cream, this dish is the ultimate Italian comfort food.
instructions: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, fry the guanciale in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy and golden. Remove from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, egg yolks, Pecorino Romano, and Parmesan. Season generously with black pepper.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining. Drain the pasta.
Working quickly, add the hot pasta to the skillet with guanciale off the heat. Toss to coat.
Pour the egg mixture over the pasta, adding a splash of reserved pasta water. Toss vigorously — the residual heat will cook the eggs into a silky sauce.
Add more pasta water as needed to achieve a creamy consistency. Season with pepper and serve immediately.
tips: Never add cream — the creaminess comes entirely from the emulsified eggs and pasta water.
Keep the heat off when adding the egg mixture to avoid scrambled eggs.
Use Pecorino Romano for authentic flavor, but Parmesan alone works in a pinch.
Work fast — the pasta needs to be hot enough to cook the eggs.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender marinated chicken in a rich, spiced tomato-cream sauce. This beloved Indian-inspired dish is full of warm spices and served best with basmati rice or naan.
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, 2 tbsp tikka spice, salt, and pepper for at least 1 hour or overnight.
Grill or broil marinated chicken until charred and cooked through, about 10–12 minutes. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté onions until golden, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger; cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in remaining tikka spice, cumin, turmeric, and garam masala. Cook 1 minute.
Add crushed tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in heavy cream and add the grilled chicken. Simmer 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with basmati rice or warm naan.
tips: Marinate the chicken overnight for maximum flavor penetration.
Charring the chicken before adding it to the sauce is key to authentic flavor.
Adjust cream quantity to control richness — coconut cream works for a dairy-free version.
Make a double batch of the sauce and freeze half for a quick weeknight dinner.

name: Avocado Toast with Poached Eggs
description: Creamy mashed avocado on crusty sourdough topped with perfectly poached eggs, chili flakes, and microgreens. A nutritious and Instagram-worthy breakfast.
ingredients: Extra virgin olive oil for drizzling
instructions: Toast sourdough slices until golden and crispy.
Halve, pit, and scoop avocados into a bowl. Mash with lemon juice, salt, and pepper to your preferred texture.
Bring a deep saucepan of water to a gentle simmer. Add white vinegar.
Crack each egg into a small cup. Create a gentle whirlpool in the water and slide eggs in one at a time.
Poach eggs for 3 minutes for a runny yolk. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towel.
Spread avocado generously on each toast slice.
Top with poached eggs. Season with chili flakes, salt, and pepper.
Garnish with microgreens, a drizzle of olive oil, and everything bagel seasoning if desired.
tips: Use very fresh eggs for the best poached egg shape.
The vinegar helps the egg whites coagulate faster for neater results.
Add a pinch of garlic powder or smoked paprika to the avocado for extra depth.
For extra crunch, sprinkle toasted pumpkin seeds on top.

name: Classic Beef Tacos
description: Seasoned ground beef in warm corn tortillas topped with shredded cheese, pico de gallo, sour cream, and fresh lime. Weeknight dinner made festive.
ingredients: 1 cup shredded cheddar or Mexican blend
instructions: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook onion until softened, 4 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add ground beef and break apart, cooking until browned.
Drain excess fat. Add all spices and stir to coat the meat.
Pour in beef broth and simmer 5 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Warm tortillas directly over a gas flame or in a dry skillet until charred in spots.
Assemble tacos: spoon beef into tortillas, top with cheese, pico de gallo, and sour cream.
Finish with a squeeze of lime and fresh cilantro.
tips: Double the spice mix and store for quick future taco nights.
Charring the tortillas slightly adds a wonderful smoky flavor.
Let guests build their own tacos at the table for a fun interactive dinner.
Add a splash of hot sauce or pickled jalapeños for heat.

name: Margherita Pizza
description: The queen of pizzas: a thin, crispy crust topped with San Marzano tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and fragrant basil leaves. Simple, elegant, perfetto.
instructions: Preheat oven to 260°C (500°F) with a pizza stone or inverted baking sheet inside.
On a floured surface, stretch or roll dough into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer dough to a semolina-dusted pizza peel or parchment paper.
Spread tomato sauce evenly, leaving a 1-inch border for the crust.
Distribute torn mozzarella pieces over the sauce.
Slide pizza onto the hot stone and bake 8–10 minutes until crust is golden and cheese bubbles.
Remove from oven, immediately scatter fresh basil leaves, drizzle with olive oil, and season.
tips: A preheated pizza stone is essential for a crispy base.
Less is more — don't overload with toppings or the crust will be soggy.
Add basil after baking to preserve its bright flavor.
Use 00 flour for an authentic Neapolitan-style dough.

name: Mango Smoothie Bowl
description: A vibrant, tropical smoothie bowl packed with frozen mango, banana, and coconut milk, topped with granola, fresh fruit, and chia seeds. Vegan and naturally sweet.
instructions: Blend frozen mango, frozen banana, and coconut milk until thick and smooth. Add lime juice.
The mixture should be very thick — add coconut milk one tablespoon at a time if needed.
Pour the smoothie base into a chilled bowl.
Arrange toppings in sections: granola on one side, fresh mango slices, blueberries.
Sprinkle chia seeds and shredded coconut over the top.
Drizzle with honey or agave if desired and serve immediately.
tips: Freeze the bowl beforehand to keep your smoothie bowl cold longer.
Use very frozen fruit for a thick, scoopable consistency.
Swap mango for acai powder and mixed berries for a purple version.
Prepare toppings the night before for a quick morning assembly.

name: Greek Salad
description: A refreshing Mediterranean salad with crisp cucumbers, juicy tomatoes, Kalamata olives, red onion, and a generous slab of creamy feta, dressed simply with olive oil and oregano.
instructions: Combine tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, and red onion in a large bowl.
Add Kalamata olives and toss gently.
Place the block of feta on top — do not crumble it.
Drizzle olive oil and red wine vinegar over the salad and feta.
Sprinkle dried oregano generously over everything.
Season with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately with crusty bread.
tips: Use block feta rather than crumbled for a more authentic, creamier experience.
Salt the cucumber slices and let them drain for 10 minutes to prevent a watery salad.
The best Greek salad uses the ripest, most flavorful summer tomatoes.
Let the salad sit for 5 minutes after dressing to allow flavors to meld.

name: Chocolate Lava Cake
description: Decadent individual chocolate cakes with a warm, gooey molten center. This elegant dessert takes only 30 minutes to make and never fails to impress.
ingredients: 150g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Grease 6 ramekins with butter and dust with cocoa powder.
Melt chocolate and butter together in a double boiler or microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk eggs, yolks, and powdered sugar until pale and slightly thickened.
Stir the chocolate mixture into the egg mixture.
Fold in flour, vanilla, and salt until just combined.
Divide batter evenly among the prepared ramekins.
Bake for exactly 12 minutes until the edges are set but the center still wobbles.
Let rest 1 minute, then run a knife around the edge and invert onto plates. Serve immediately with ice cream.
tips: Test one cake first — oven temperatures vary and timing is critical.
Batter can be made 24 hours ahead and refrigerated; add 1–2 minutes to baking time.
Use the best quality dark chocolate you can find — it makes all the difference.
The cakes should feel set on the outside but jiggle in the center when done.

name: Pad Thai
description: Thailand's most famous street food — stir-fried rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, eggs, and bean sprouts in a sweet-tangy tamarind sauce, topped with crushed peanuts.
ingredients: 2 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar
Lime wedges and dried chili flakes to serve
instructions: Soak rice noodles in cold water for 30 minutes until pliable. Drain.
Mix tamarind paste, fish sauce, and sugar to make the sauce. Taste and adjust balance.
Heat wok over very high heat. Add 1 tbsp oil and stir-fry shrimp until pink. Remove.
Add another tbsp oil and fry tofu until golden on all sides. Remove.
Add remaining oil and garlic; stir-fry 30 seconds.
Add drained noodles and sauce. Toss and stir-fry 2 minutes.
Push noodles to side, crack in eggs, scramble briefly, then mix into noodles.
Add shrimp, tofu, and half the bean sprouts. Toss everything together.
Serve topped with crushed peanuts, remaining sprouts, spring onions, lime, and chili flakes.
tips: High heat is non-negotiable — it gives the noodles the characteristic wok-charred flavor.
Don't crowd the wok; cook in batches if needed.
Soak noodles in cold water, not hot — they finish cooking in the wok.
Adjust the sauce ratio to your taste: more tamarind for tang, more sugar for sweetness.

name: Classic Pancakes
description: Fluffy, golden American-style pancakes made from scratch. Light, airy, and perfectly stacked — serve with maple syrup and fresh berries for the ultimate breakfast.
ingredients: 3 tbsp melted butter, plus extra for pan
Maple syrup and fresh berries to serve
instructions: Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, and salt in a large bowl.
In another bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour wet ingredients into dry ingredients and stir until just combined — lumps are fine.
Rest the batter for 5 minutes. Do not overmix.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Brush with butter.
Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form and edges look set, about 2 minutes.
Flip and cook another 1–2 minutes until golden.
Serve in a stack with maple syrup, butter, and fresh berries.
tips: Don't overmix — lumpy batter makes fluffier pancakes.
The pan is ready when a drop of water dances and evaporates on contact.
Only flip once: wait until bubbles cover the entire surface.
Keep finished pancakes warm in a 90°C (200°F) oven while you cook the batch.

name: Caesar Salad
description: Crisp romaine lettuce tossed in a bold, garlicky Caesar dressing with shaved Parmesan and golden croutons. A timeless salad that doubles as a satisfying lunch.
instructions: Make dressing: pound garlic and anchovies into a paste using a mortar and pestle.
Whisk in Dijon, lemon juice, and Worcestershire sauce.
Whisk in mayonnaise and olive oil until smooth. Season generously.
Make croutons: toss cubed bread with olive oil, garlic powder, and salt. Toast at 190°C for 12 minutes.
Place romaine in a large bowl. Add three-quarters of the dressing and toss to coat.
Add croutons and half the Parmesan. Toss gently.
Plate and top with remaining Parmesan. Drizzle more dressing if desired.
tips: Make the dressing up to 3 days ahead and refrigerate.
Dry the romaine thoroughly after washing — wet lettuce dilutes the dressing.
Add grilled chicken or shrimp to turn this into a complete meal.
Use freshly grated Parmesan, not the pre-shredded variety, for best flavor.

name: Vegetable Stir Fry
description: A colorful medley of crisp vegetables tossed in a savory ginger-soy sauce. Ready in 20 minutes, this vegan stir fry is a weeknight hero served over steamed rice.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and spring onions to garnish
instructions: Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and cornstarch to make sauce. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add carrot and broccoli; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add bell peppers, snap peas, and mushrooms. Stir-fry 3 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour sauce over vegetables and toss to coat. Cook until sauce thickens, about 1 minute.
Serve over steamed rice, garnished with sesame seeds and spring onions.
tips: Prep all vegetables before you start — stir-frying moves fast.
High heat is essential for the smoky 'wok hei' flavor.
Don't overcrowd the wok or vegetables will steam instead of fry.
Use tamari instead of soy sauce to make this dish gluten-free.

name: Beef Burger
description: A juicy, smash-style beef burger with melted American cheese, crispy lettuce, ripe tomato, pickles, and special sauce on a toasted brioche bun. Diner-quality at home.
instructions: Mix mayo, ketchup, mustard, and pickle juice to make special sauce. Refrigerate.
Divide beef into 4 equal balls. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat a cast iron skillet over high heat until very hot.
Add a ball of beef and smash it flat with a spatula. Cook 2 minutes until a crust forms.
Flip, immediately add cheese, and cook 1 more minute.
Toast buns cut-side down in butter until golden.
Build burgers: sauce on both bun halves, lettuce, tomato, onion, pickle, and patty.
tips: Don't overwork the beef — handle it as little as possible for a tender patty.
The cast iron skillet must be screaming hot for a proper smash crust.
Season the beef RIGHT before cooking, not before — it changes texture.
Let the cheese melt fully by covering the pan for 30 seconds.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a richly spiced tomato and pepper sauce, topped with crumbled feta and fresh herbs. A North African and Middle Eastern classic perfect for any meal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté onion and pepper until soft, 8 minutes.
Add garlic, tomato paste, and all spices. Cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Crush in the tomatoes with your hands or a spoon. Season with salt and pepper.
Simmer sauce for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover and cook on low heat 7–10 minutes until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Scatter feta over the top and garnish with fresh herbs. Serve directly from the pan.
tips: Cook the tomatoes long enough to remove the raw taste — at least 15 minutes.
Shake the pan gently to check if the egg whites are set.
Add harissa paste for extra heat and depth.
Serve straight from the pan for the full experience.

name: Tiramisu
description: Italy's iconic coffee dessert: layers of espresso-soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone cream dusted with cocoa. No baking required, and it gets better as it sits overnight.
ingredients: 2 tbsp coffee liqueur (Kahlúa or Tia Maria)
instructions: Whisk egg yolks and sugar until pale and thick, about 5 minutes.
Beat in mascarpone and vanilla until smooth.
In a separate bowl, whisk egg whites to stiff peaks.
Fold egg whites gently into the mascarpone mixture in three additions.
Mix espresso and coffee liqueur in a shallow dish.
Quickly dip each ladyfinger into the espresso (1 second per side) and arrange in a single layer in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers.
Add another layer of dipped ladyfingers and top with remaining cream.
Dust generously with cocoa powder. Refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
tips: Don't soak the ladyfingers too long — a quick dip prevents a soggy texture.
Use room temperature mascarpone for a lump-free cream.
Overnight refrigeration is ideal — it firms up beautifully.
For a non-alcoholic version, simply omit the liqueur.

name: Guacamole
description: Fresh, vibrant guacamole made with ripe Hass avocados, lime, jalapeño, cilantro, and red onion. The ultimate snack and party dip — no additives, just real ingredients.
instructions: Halve and pit avocados. Scoop flesh into a bowl.
Add lime juice immediately to prevent browning.
Mash avocado to your preferred texture — chunky or smooth.
Fold in red onion, jalapeño, garlic, cilantro, and tomato.
Season with salt and cumin. Taste and adjust lime/salt as needed.
Cover tightly with plastic wrap pressed directly on the surface to prevent oxidation.
Serve within 2 hours for best color, accompanied by tortilla chips.
tips: Ripe avocados are essential — they should yield to gentle pressure.
Leave one avocado pit in the bowl to slow browning.
Soak red onion in cold water for 5 minutes to mellow its sharpness.
Make it your own — some people love it chunky, others prefer it silky.

name: Butter Chicken
description: Mildly spiced, velvety tomato-butter sauce enveloping tender chicken pieces. This North Indian classic is rich, aromatic, and pairs perfectly with garlic naan.
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, tandoori masala, and salt for 2 hours.
Grill or bake marinated chicken at 220°C for 20 minutes until slightly charred.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Cook onion puree until golden.
Add garlic and ginger; cook 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, chili powder, and sugar. Simmer 15 minutes.
Blend the sauce until smooth with an immersion blender.
Add grilled chicken and garam masala. Simmer 10 minutes.
Stir in cream, adjust seasoning, and garnish with cilantro.
tips: Kashmiri chili gives the sauce its characteristic deep red color without too much heat.
Blending the sauce to silky smoothness is the key to restaurant-quality results.
Add a small piece of dried fenugreek (kasuri methi) at the end for authentic flavor.
Butter chicken tastes even better the next day as flavors meld.

name: Banana Bread
description: Moist, tender banana bread with a rich banana flavor and golden crust. Made with overripe bananas, this beloved quick bread is perfect for breakfast or afternoon snack.
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease a 9x5 inch loaf pan.
Mash bananas well in a large bowl until smooth.
Stir in melted butter until combined.
Mix in sugar, beaten egg, and vanilla extract.
Sprinkle in baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. Stir.
Fold in flour until just combined — do not overmix.
Fold in walnuts if using.
Pour into prepared pan and bake 60–65 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool in pan for 10 minutes before turning out onto a rack.
tips: The blacker and spottier the bananas, the sweeter and more flavorful the bread.
Overmixing develops gluten and makes the bread tough — stir until just combined.
Add chocolate chips for an indulgent twist.
Wrap cooled loaf in plastic wrap — it gets more moist the next day.

name: Caprese Salad
description: Alternating slices of ripe heirloom tomatoes and fresh buffalo mozzarella drizzled with extra virgin olive oil and balsamic glaze, finished with fresh basil.
ingredients: 2 tbsp aged balsamic vinegar or balsamic glaze
instructions: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into equal-thickness rounds (about 1cm).
Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a platter.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between and around the slices.
Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil.
Drizzle balsamic glaze in a zigzag pattern over the top.
Finish with flaky sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper.
Serve immediately at room temperature — do not refrigerate assembled salad.
tips: This salad lives and dies by ingredient quality — use the best tomatoes and mozzarella you can find.
Serve at room temperature, never cold — refrigeration dulls the flavors.
A good aged balsamic (10+ years) is thick enough to drizzle without making a glaze.
Tear rather than cut the basil leaves to release more aroma.

name: Vegan Lentil Soup
description: Hearty, warming red lentil soup infused with cumin, turmeric, and smoked paprika. A nutritious, protein-packed vegan soup that comes together in under 45 minutes.
ingredients: Fresh parsley and crusty bread to serve
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion, carrot, and celery until softened, 8 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 2 minutes.
Add cumin, turmeric, paprika, and cayenne. Stir and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, crushed tomatoes, and vegetable broth. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook 25 minutes until lentils break down.
Use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup for a creamy-chunky texture.
Stir in lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Serve garnished with fresh parsley and a drizzle of olive oil.
tips: Red lentils don't need soaking and break down quickly into a creamy texture.
The lemon juice at the end brightens all the flavors — don't skip it.
This soup thickens as it sits; add broth when reheating.
Stir in a handful of spinach in the last 2 minutes for extra nutrition.
